description_long: This is a roughly hand-shaped, bulbous clay vase with a wide, rounded body and a slightly flared rim. The surface is matte and uneven, showing visible textural variations and small imperfections typical of traditional pottery. One side of the vase features a prominent crack and some surface abrasions, suggesting age or wear. The color is a muted, earthy gray with subtle speckling throughout, giving it a natural, unglazed appearance. The opening is wide enough to accommodate medium-sized floral arrangements or decorative branches. The overall form is simple and utilitarian, with no visible handles or ornate embellishments.
description: A bulbous, matte gray clay vase with a flared rim, visible surface imperfections, and a prominent crack on one side. Its simple, rustic form suggests traditional handcrafting.
description_view_0: The vase appears rounded with a slightly flared rim and a smooth, subtly textured surface; no major cracks are visible from this angle.
description_view_1: This view highlights a large crack and surface abrasions on the upper body, making it the most visibly damaged side.
description_view_2: The vase looks uniformly rounded and smooth, with no visible cracks or major imperfections from this angle.
description_view_3: A mostly smooth, rounded surface is visible, with minor dimples and no significant cracks or damage.
